steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
You are a log analyst investigating access patterns on a web server. There is a directory at /home/user/server_logs containing several Apache-style access log files from different dates.

Your task is to find all log entries across all .log files in the /home/user/server_logs directory that contain HTTP 404 error responses and save them to a single consolidated file.

Specifically:
1. Search through all files ending in .log within /home/user/server_logs
2. Find all lines that contain the pattern " 404 " (the HTTP status code 404 surrounded by spaces)
3. Save all matching lines to a new file at /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt

The output file should contain the raw matching lines exactly as they appear in the original log files, with each match on its own line. The file should be created even if no matches are found (in which case it would be empty).

Make sure the /home/user/analysis directory exists before writing the output file.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user#

Analysis: I'm at the /home/user directory in a Linux terminal. I need to search for HTTP 404 errors in Apache-style access log files located in /home/user/server_logs and save them to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt.
Plan: 1. First, create the /home/user/analysis directory if it doesn't exist using mkdir -p
2. Use grep to search for ' 404 ' pattern in all .log files in /home/user/server_logs
3. Redirect the output to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
4. Verify the file was created

results.content:
Previous response had warnings:
WARNINGS: - Extra text detected before JSON object
- Extra text detected after JSON object

New Terminal Output:

root@383fb8b37129:/home/user# mkdir -p /home/user/analysis
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user# grep ' 404 ' /home/user/server_logs/*.log > /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt 2>/dev/null || touch /home/user/analysis/404_errors
.txt
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user# ls -la /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 519 Apr 15 18:01 /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user# wc -l /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
4 /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user#

steps.message:
Analysis: The task has been completed successfully. I created the /home/user/analysis directory and used grep to search for ' 404 ' pattern in all .log files in /home/user/server_logs. The results were saved to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt. The file exists (519 bytes) and contains 4 matching lines with 404 errors.
Plan: Let me verify the content of the output file to confirm it contains the expected 404 error log entries, then mark the task as complete.

results.content:
New Terminal Output:

root@383fb8b37129:/home/user# cat /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
/home/user/server_logs/access_2024_01_15.log:192.168.1.101 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:16:45 +0000] "GET /missing-page.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
/home/user/server_logs/access_2024_01_15.log:192.168.1.103 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:18:01 +0000] "GET /old-link.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
/home/user/server_logs/access_2024_01_16.log:10.0.0.51 - - [16/Jan/2024:08:31:22 +0000] "GET /nonexistent HTTP/1.1" 404 512
/home/user/server_logs/access_2024_01_17.log:172.16.0.26 - - [17/Jan/2024:14:21:30 +0000] "GET /deleted-resource HTTP/1.1" 404 512
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user#

steps.message:
Analysis: The task is complete. The file /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt has been created and contains 4 log entries with HTTP 404 errors from multiple log files:
- 2 entries from access_2024_01_15.log (missing-page.html and old-link.html)
- 1 entry from access_2024_01_16.log (nonexistent)
- 1 entry from access_2024_01_17.log (deleted-resource)

The grep command included the filename prefix in each line. The task requirements asked for raw matching lines. Let me fix this by running grep without the filename prefix using the -h option.
Plan: Re-run grep with -h flag to exclude filename prefixes and get only the raw matching lines as specified in the requirements.

results.content:
New Terminal Output:

root@383fb8b37129:/home/user# grep -h ' 404 ' /home/user/server_logs/*.log > /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user# cat /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
192.168.1.101 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:16:45 +0000] "GET /missing-page.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
192.168.1.103 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:18:01 +0000] "GET /old-link.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512
10.0.0.51 - - [16/Jan/2024:08:31:22 +0000] "GET /nonexistent HTTP/1.1" 404 512
172.16.0.26 - - [17/Jan/2024:14:21:30 +0000] "GET /deleted-resource HTTP/1.1" 404 512
root@383fb8b37129:/home/user#

steps.message:
Analysis: The task is now fully complete. The file /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt contains exactly the raw matching lines from the original log files, without filename prefixes. It has 4 HTTP 404 error entries:
1. 192.168.1.101 requesting /missing-page.html
2. 192.168.1.103 requesting /old-link.html
3. 10.0.0.51 requesting /nonexistent
4. 172.16.0.26 requesting /deleted-resource

All requirements have been met:
- The /home/user/analysis directory was created
- All .log files in /home/user/server_logs were searched
- Lines containing ' 404 ' pattern were found
- Results were saved to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
- The output contains raw matching lines exactly as they appear in the original logs
Plan: Task is complete. No further actions needed.

Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.

steps.message:
Analysis: Yes, I'm confident the task is complete. I have:
1. Created the /home/user/analysis directory
2. Searched all .log files in /home/user/server_logs for the pattern ' 404 '
3. Saved all 4 matching lines to /home/user/analysis/404_errors.txt
4. The output file contains the raw matching lines exactly as they appear in the original log files

All requirements have been fulfilled.
Plan: Confirm task completion.
